records.ety: [OE. fadme, faðme, AS. fæðm fathom, the embracing arms; akin to OS. faðmos the outstretched arms, D. vadem, vaam, fathom, OHG. fadom, fadum, G. faden fathom, thread, Icel. faðmr fathom, Sw. famn, Dan. favn; cf. Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/ to spread out, <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/ outspread, flat, L. patere to lie open, extend. Cf. Patent, Petal.]

defs: A measure of length, containing six feet; the space to which a man can extend his arms; -- used chiefly in measuring cables, cordage, and the depth of navigable water by soundings.
The measure or extant of one's capacity; depth, as of intellect; profundity; reach; penetration.
quotes: Another of his fathom they have none To lead their business.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fathomed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Fathoming.]

defs: To encompass with the arms extended or encircling; to measure by throwing the arms about; to span.
To measure by a sounding line; especially, to sound the depth of; to penetrate, measure, and comprehend; to get to the bottom of.
quotes: The page of life that was spread out before me seemed dull and commonplace, only because I had not fathomed its deeper import.
